summary: Russian forces launched a massive Shahed drone barrage of 124 UAVs on 22‑23 May, striking multiple sectors in the east and south. Russia also announced the recapture of Verkhnyaya Tersa in Zaporizhzhia, extending its foothold in the region. Ukrainian civilian infrastructure suffered notable damage, including a college attack in Starobelsk with 39 casualties and a missile strike in Dnipro wounding five.
key_points: 124 Shahed and other UAVs attacked Ukrainian positions from multiple directions, including Kursk, Shatalovo, and Orel, on the night of 21‑22 May (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian Ministry of Defense reported the liberation of Verkhnyaya Tersa in Zaporizhzhia Oblast (Source: @tass_agency/377047).
Ukrainian attack on Starobelsk college (LNR) collapsed the building, killing 4 and injuring 39 total (Source: @tass_agency/377041).
Five civilians injured in Dnipro after a Russian strike damaged two high‑rise buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
US approved a $108 million sale of Hawk air‑defence systems to Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81377).
Ukrainian FPV drone units continue operating despite funding shortfalls, seeking small public donations (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).
Multiple UAV sightings reported across central Ukraine: Shahed‑type drones over Sumy‑Poltava corridor, Kremenchuk, Myrhorod, and Mykolaiv (Source: @kpszsu/62412, @kpszsu/62408, @kpszsu/62434).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions (Source: @kpszsu/62437).

sections.executive_summary: A coordinated Russian UAV barrage of 124 Shahed drones struck eastern and southern fronts on 22‑23 May, causing widespread disruption. Russia announced the recapture of Verkhnyaya Tersa, consolidating its position in Zaporizhzhia. Ukraine suffered significant civilian casualties, notably at a Starobelsk college (39 injured) and in Dnipro (five wounded).

points: Five civilians, aged 34‑78, were injured and four hospitalized after a Russian missile hit two high‑rise buildings in Dnipro, highlighting ongoing urban targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
Ukrainian FPV drone units continue combat operations despite limited funding, soliciting public donations of 5‑10 UAH per person to sustain drone procurement (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).

Ukrainian forces reported multiple Shahed‑type UAV incursions over central regions: Sumy‑Poltava corridor, Kremenchuk from the southeast, and Mykolaiv from the south, indicating expanded enemy air‑space penetration (Source: @kpszsu/62412, @kpszsu/62408, @kpszsu/62434).
Ukrainian air‑defence received a $108 million Hawk system package from the United States, enhancing medium‑range coverage against low‑altitude threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81377).
Ukrainian military competitions for UAV operators in Truskavets showcased new tactics and rewarded top FPV crews, reflecting ongoing skill development (Source: @DeepStateUA/23528).
The Russian Ministry of Defence confirmed the liberation of Verkhnyaya Tersa in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, expanding Russian control along the southern front (Source: @tass_agency/377047).

A massive UAV strike involving 124 Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and imitation drones was launched from multiple directions, targeting Ukrainian positions across the east and south (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs on Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions, indicating intensified use of precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/62437).
Russian forces continue to employ Shahed‑type drones from bases in Kursk, Shatalovo, Orel, and other western districts, demonstrating deep‑strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian propaganda highlighted alleged torture of Ukrainian POWs, though no direct operational impact was reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81384).
Shahed UAVs attacked from Kursk, Shatalovo, and Orel, striking eastern Ukrainian positions and causing disruptions to command‑and‑control networks (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian reports of UAV activity over Sumy heading toward Poltava suggest Russian reconnaissance and strike intent in the northeastern axis (Source: @kpszsu/62401).
The Starobelsk college attack in LNR resulted in 39 casualties, underscoring the vulnerability of civilian infrastructure in the contested eastern zone (Source: @tass_agency/377041).
Ukrainian forces engaged Russian UAVs over Kharkiv, with tactical aviation responding with guided bombs to suppress enemy air activity (Source: @kpszsu/62437).
Ukrainian FPV drone units continue low‑cost operations in the east, seeking public micro‑donations to sustain sorties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).
Ukrainian authorities reported increased UAV sightings from Sumy toward Poltava, indicating potential Russian pressure on the northern‑central axis (Source: @kpszsu/62401).
No major ground engagements were reported, but Russian statements about Belarusian‑Russian exercises raise concerns of future northern escalation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30344).
Ukrainian border security measures were heightened in the north following disinformation about Belarusian troop buildup (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30344).
Ukrainian air‑defence upgrades, including the Hawk system, aim to counter low‑altitude threats along the northern frontier (Source: @UkraineNow/81377).
Russian UAVs launched from western districts continue to probe northern Ukrainian airspace, though no significant damage reported (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Ukrainian reports of Shahed‑type drones approaching Kremenchuk from the southeast highlight ongoing pressure on the southern central corridor (Source: @kpszsu/62412).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Zaporizhzhia, targeting infrastructure and troop concentrations (Source: @kpszsu/62437).
The recapture of Verkhnyaya Tersa strengthens Russian foothold near the southern front, potentially threatening nearby supply routes (Source: @tass_agency/377047).
Ukrainian civilian casualties in Dnipro from a missile strike underscore the vulnerability of southern urban centers (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
Ukrainian UAV units continue low‑cost FPV operations in the south, seeking public micro‑donations for sustainment (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).
A coordinated wave of 124 Shahed and other UAVs struck Ukrainian positions across multiple fronts, representing the largest single UAV barrage reported this month (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
Russian tactical aviation employed guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, indicating a shift toward precision strike tactics (Source: @kpszsu/62437).
Ukrainian air‑defence received a $108 million Hawk system package from the United States, enhancing medium‑range coverage against low‑altitude threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81377).
Ukrainian FPV drone units remain active despite funding constraints, relying on micro‑donations to maintain sortie rates (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).

Multiple UAV sightings across central Ukraine (Sumy‑Poltava, Kremenchuk, Mykolaiv) suggest expanded Russian aerial reconnaissance and strike envelope (Source: @kpszsu/62412, @kpszsu/62408, @kpszsu/62434).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive Shahed UAV barrage of 124 drones from Kursk, Shatalovo, Orel and other directions, striking eastern and southern Ukrainian positions on 22‑23 May (Source: @kpszsu/62415).
The Russian Ministry of Defence announced the liberation of Verkhnyaya Tersa in Zaporizhzhia Oblast, extending Russian control in the south (Source: @tass_agency/377047).
A Ukrainian strike on a college in Starobelsk (LNR) collapsed the building, killing four and injuring 39 civilians (Source: @tass_agency/377041).
Five civilians were injured in Dnipro after a Russian missile hit two high‑rise buildings, highlighting continued urban targeting (Source: @UkraineNow/81369).
The United States approved a $108 million sale of Hawk air‑defence systems to Ukraine, bolstering its low‑altitude defense capability (Source: @UkraineNow/81377).
Ukrainian FPV drone units continue combat operations despite limited funding, soliciting public micro‑donations to sustain drone sorties (Source: @mon1tor_ua/66894).
Russian tactical aviation deployed guided aerial bombs over Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia, indicating intensified precision strike use (Source: @kpszsu/62437).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are escalating aerial pressure through large‑scale UAV barrages and precision bombings, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure across the eastern and southern fronts. The recapture of Verkhnyaya Tersa strengthens Russian footholds near key supply routes, while continued UAV incursions in central Ukraine expand the threat envelope. Ukrainian air‑defence improvements, such as the Hawk system, may mitigate low‑altitude attacks but will be tested against the high volume of incoming drones.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering its air‑defence capabilities with Western aid, notably the US Hawk system, while relying on domestic FPV drone units funded by public donations to maintain operational tempo. Internationally, the US is increasing military support, whereas Russian propaganda seeks to amplify alleged Ukrainian aggression. Civilian resilience remains strained as urban centers like Dnipro endure repeated strikes.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch sites in western Russian districts is critical, as the recent 124‑drone barrage demonstrated coordinated long‑range strike capability. The newly secured Verkhnyaya Tersa should be observed for potential logistics hubs supporting further southern offensives. Ukrainian FPV drone activity, though low‑cost, provides valuable reconnaissance and should be integrated into broader ISR networks.
